changes_summary: Revised Step 2 to use structural schema comparison (`JSON.stringify`) instead of dropping schema from dedup — addresses FLAG-001's evidence that schema can change independently of code via EffectCreatorPanel. Added `schemasEqual` helper and test coverage for structural dedup. Acknowledged FLAG-002's render-purity concern in Step 3 — the guard is the right pragmatic fix since registry is stable and children need synchronous visibility on first mount; no scope creep into useEffect refactoring.

success_criteria.criterion: validateAndCoerceParams preserves params not defined in the schema while still validating/coercing schema-defined ones
register() and registerAsync() dedup correctly when the same schema is passed as a structurally-equal but referentially-distinct object
register() and registerAsync() still detect and apply schema-only changes (same code, different schema)
replaceEffectRegistry is only called when the registry instance actually changes, not on every render
EffectLayerSequence does not wrap children in an inner Sequence when startFrame is 0
All existing tests in DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx pass (updated as needed)
No unnecessary defensive code or abstractions added beyond the minimal fixes

assumptions: ParameterSchema arrays are plain JSON-serializable data, so JSON.stringify comparison is reliable for structural equality.
Extra (non-schema) params in validateAndCoerceParams are intentional and used by effects — not stale junk that should be stripped.
Remotion Sequence with from={0} is functionally identical to no wrapper, so skipping it is safe.

The initial-mount render-time side effect in useEffectRegistry is intentional — children need the singleton registry set synchronously before their first render. A full useEffect migration would break child visibility timing and is out of scope.
